French Power Prices Jump as Hot Weather Spurs Nuclear Concerns
items.summary: French power prices soared on concerns that scorching summer temperatures will lead to potential nuclear curtailments as well as lower hydro availability.

items.title: ECB Set to Lift Its Inflation Outlook in June, Lane Tells Nikkei
items.summary: The European Central Bank will probably raise its quarterly projection for inflation next month as the Iran war keeps energy prices elevated, Chief Economist Philip Lane told Nikkei in remarks published Monday.

items.title: Brazil’s Cotton Farmers Cash in on Rally Fueled by Obstacles Worlds Away
items.summary: Brazilian cotton farmers and traders are ready to profit from a rally fueled by two disparate events: the Iran war and drought.

items.title: Ex-Credit Suisse Risk Chief Has Swiss Criminal Case Dismissed
items.summary: Credit Suisse’s former head of compliance has avoided a criminal trial for allegedly ignoring red flags in a scandal that helped hasten the bank’s collapse, after the case was dismissed on technical grounds.

items.title: Powell Will Vote With His Mind and Heart, Daco Says
items.summary: Gregory Daco, EY-Parthenon chief economist, says he thinks former Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell will not be afraid to dissent as a member of the board of governors. Daco speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: JPMorgan’s Matejka Sees Stocks Overpricing Risk of Rate Hikes
items.summary: Markets are overpricing potential central bank interest-rate increases, creating conditions for a rally in stocks with the lowest volatility like staples and utilities, according to strategists at JPMorgan Chase & Co.

RT by @mfa_russia: Dmitry #Polyanskiy at the roundtable“Collaborationism: Lessons Not Learned” (25 May 2026): 

• After World War II, Nazism and fascism were universally condemned, andattempts to whitewash Nazi criminals or question the outcome of the war were unacceptable. While the Nuremberg Tribunal condemned German Nazism and its leadership, many “small nazisms” - movements that followed Nazi ideology - avoided similar condemnation.

• Today Ukraine overtly advocates for historical revisionism and the glorification of Nazi collaborators, including Yaroslav Stetsko, Stepan Bandera, Andrei Melnyk, Roman Shukhevich and the Nachtigal Battalion. The notorious role of the Ukrainian nationalists in the 1941 Lvov pogroms is well known. But still Kiev names streets after them.

items.title: Europe Condemns Russia  Oreshnik  Missile Strike as Escalation
Türkiye marks 66 years since 1960 military coup , executions

Today in Kyiv, a Summit is taking place that has brought together 24 countries, primarily from Europe. All of our European neighbors are represented: Poland, Slovakia, Hungary, Moldova, Romania, and Belarus. 

We all support the aspiration of the Belarusian people to free themselves from Russian interference, and we know that Russia is now trying to drag Belarus even deeper into this war against Ukraine. We value every expression of support from Belarusians for a free Ukraine, and we know that the day will come when there will once again be good-neighborly relations between our states – based on the real independence of both Ukraine and Belarus from Moscow. 

This is exactly what we discussed today with Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya and her team, who are visiting Kyiv. Ukraine has never been a threat to Belarus. And we are grateful to those Belarusians who stand with Ukraine – now, when the fate of both our independence and the independence of every nation that borders Russia is being decided.

items.title:
It is important now to continue conveying to all political leaders that air defense for Ukraine is the number one priority. It is important to continue working together on the resilience of Ukrainian communities – and therefore on the experience of resilience that will be useful to communities in your countries.

It is enormously helpful for Ukraine to feel that we are not alone in our defense. People in your countries, as well as here in Ukraine, must continue to feel that evil will not prevail.

And we will remain very grateful to you for every joint project, every strong decision, every step that helps our defense and the protection of our cities and villages from Russian strikes, and for your participation in Ukraine’s reconstruction.

The fate of our freedom and yours is being decided here, in Ukraine, in this war – in which system prevails here: our shared European system, or Moscow’s.

From the speech at the 4th International Summit of Cities and Regions. (3/3)

items.title: US Natural Gas Rises on Lower Output, Higher Flows to LNG Plants
items.summary: US natural gas futures increased as producers reduced output over the US holiday weekend and more domestic gas supplies flowed to liquefied natural gas export terminals on the Gulf Coast. Some of those facilities had previously lowered capacity to perform seasonal maintenance, pushing more gas into the domestic market.

items.title: Stocks Gain on Optimism Over US-Iran Deal to Open Hormuz
items.summary: Equities climbed and US oil prices were lower as investors bet that a deal to re-open the Strait of Hormuz was close, even as hostilities flared to test the fragile ceasefire.

items.title: Oil Rebounds After US and Iran Clash Near Hormuz | Bloomberg Brief 5/26/2026
items.summary: Brent crude rebounds after US and Israeli jets struck Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after President Trump suggested peace talks were progressing. Katrina Dudley of Franklin Templeton discusses inflation risks and consumer pressure. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: US, Iran Clash Near Hormuz as Both Tout Progress on Talks
items.summary: US and Iranian forces clashed near the Strait of Hormuz overnight, hours after US President Donald Trump reiterated that negotiations with Tehran to extend their ceasefire and reopen the strait were proceeding, and just before a published statement from Iran’s Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

I met with Édouard Philippe, Mayor of the French city of Le Havre and leader of the Horizons political party. I am grateful for his support for our people, and this visit in particular is an important demonstration of solidarity with Ukraine, especially after Russia’s recent massive attacks against our country.

We discussed Ukraine’s movement toward membership in the European Union. We value France’s consistent support on this matter. We expect all negotiating clusters to be opened in the very near future. Special attention was paid to energy assistance and defense cooperation. Our priority is to strengthen Ukraine’s air defense and develop our own European anti-ballistic capabilities.

I thank France for its support and constant attention to Ukraine’s security situation and European future.

items.title:
R to @ZelenskyyUa: Зустрівся з мером французького міста Гавр, головою політичної партії «Горизонти» Едуаром Філіппом. Вдячний за підтримку нашого народу, і зокрема цей візит є важливим проявом солідарності з Україною, особливо після нещодавніх російських масованих атак по нашій країні.

Говорили про наш рух до членства у Євросоюзі. Цінуємо послідовну підтримку Франції в цьому питанні. Ми розраховуємо на відкриття всіх переговорних кластерів уже найближчим часом. Окрема увага – енергетичній допомозі та співпраці в оборонній сфері. Наш пріоритет – посилити протиповітряну оборону України та створити власну європейську антибалістику.

Дякую Франції за підтримку й постійну увагу до безпекової ситуації та європейського майбутнього України.
